description_long: This is a handheld stapler with a robust, industrial design, featuring a metallic body with a dark, weathered finish. The main body is rectangular with a large, open handle for gripping, and a long, brown metallic lever extends from the top, likely used for loading staples. The stapler has a visible trigger mechanism on one side, and a small, round metallic fastener or pin is present near the hinge area. The staple exit slot is located at the front end of the stapler, where the brown lever meets the main body. The overall appearance suggests durability and functionality, suitable for heavy-duty office or workshop use.
description: A heavy-duty, dark metallic stapler with a large handle and a brown lever, designed for robust office or workshop tasks.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular staple exit slot and the alignment of the brown lever with the main body.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the open handle, trigger mechanism, and the full length of the brown lever.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the handle and the top of the stapler, with the lever extending forward.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the handle, trigger, and the brown lever from the other side.
attribute_handle_design: The handle is rectangular with rounded edges, featuring a large open space for gripping. It has a matte black finish, appears to be made of metal or hard plastic, and lacks visible ergonomic padding or texturing.
attribute_metal_hinge_mechanism: The metal hinge mechanism is located at the top rear of the stapler, connecting the brown lever to the main body. It consists of a visible metallic pin or fastener, allowing the lever to pivot for staple loading and operation.
attribute_staple_exit_slot: The staple exit slot is positioned at the front end of the stapler, where the brown lever meets the main body. It is circular and clearly visible in the front view, designed for staples to be ejected downward.
